full_prompt: Design a 6U CubeSat primary structure benchmark for a constellation member, compliant with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 for 6U deployers (12U / 24U capable P-PODs) and carrying deployable solar arrays. The structure is a skeletonized Al 7075-T6 chassis with external envelope 226.3 mm x 100.0 mm x 366.0 mm (6U standard), four deployer rails on the long 366.0 mm corners, and a total mass cap of 12.0 kg per CDS Rev 14 6U with 14 kg absolute cap. The structure must support two deployable solar arrays each 340 x 200 mm x 1.5 mm (triple-junction GaAs, 250 g each) folded against the long face during launch and deployed after separation to 45 degrees off-axis, plus an internal PC/104+ stack of 12 boards. Qualification loads per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000 / NASA-STD-5001B: (LC1) quasi-static 15 g axial + 10 g lateral, (LC2) sine vibration 2.5 g at 5-100 Hz, (LC3) random vibration GRMS 14.1 at 20-2000 Hz, (LC4) shock 1000 g peak 100-10000 Hz, (LC5) thermal vacuum -40 to +70 C LEO thermal cycle. Material 7075-T6 (yield 503 MPa, E 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Pass/fail: (R1) quasi-static combined load max von Mises must not exceed 251 MPa (yield 503 / SF 2.0), (R2) first natural frequency stowed must be at least 80 Hz per GEVS secondary payload, (R3) total mass at or below 12 kg, (R4) thermal cycle combined stress at hot case (+70 C) plus 15 g axial must not exceed 251 MPa, (R5) solar array tip deflection when deployed and operating at 45 degrees under 1g ground-test must not exceed 5 mm (reality check for hinge stiffness). Vibration (modal) is required for R2. Thermal is required for R4. Fluid and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with chassis, solar array panels (stowed), hinges, and PC/104+ cage as sub-bodies plus a structural + thermal report.
short_prompt:
Design a 6U CubeSat primary structure for a constellation member, to Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 (6U / 12U / 24U deployer family), in 7075-T6 aluminum. Envelope 226.3 x 100.0 x 366.0 mm with four deployer rails on the long corners and a mass cap of 12.0 kg (14 kg absolute). Two deployable solar arrays (each 340 x 200 x 1.5 mm, ~250 g, triple-junction GaAs) stow flat against the long face during launch and deploy to 45 deg off-axis; internal PC/104+ stack of 12 boards. Rails are constrained in the P-POD; stowed arrays are clamped to the long face.

Qualification per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000 / NASA-STD-5001B.

Load cases.
- LC1 quasi-static: 15 g axial + 10 g lateral.
- LC2 sine vibration: 2.5 g, 5-100 Hz.
- LC3 random vibration: 14.1 GRMS, 20-2000 Hz.
- LC4 shock: 1000 g peak, 100-10000 Hz.
- LC5 thermal vacuum: -40 to +70 C LEO cycle.

First natural frequency (stowed) must be >=80 Hz per GEVS; solar-array tip deflection when deployed at 45 deg under 1 g ground-test is also checked.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Multi-sat benefit justification requires a constellation-level link and coverage budget, outside FEA.

prompt.title: 6U constellation CubeSat primary structure
prompt.brief: Design a 7075-T6 6U CubeSat structure (226.3 x 100 x 366 mm, <=12 kg) with 2 deployable solar arrays passing GEVS quasi-static 15 g + 10 g, modal >=80 Hz, thermal -40/+70 C, and 1 g ground-deployment deflection.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Chassis stiffness dominated by modal floor.
